properties.text: A severe thunderstorm moving northeast toward Valence is producing intense rainfall, frequent lightning, and a powerful convective core with reflectivity repeatedly reaching 62–65 dBZ. These values indicate a strong, sustained updraft capable of generating hail up to 3 cm, along with the potential for brief damaging wind gusts near the strongest cells. The storm remains well‑organized and continues advancing toward the lower Rhône valley with severe hazards possible along its path.

properties.text: A small thunderstorm cell with reflectivity around 48 dBZ is moving north-northeast toward the Mělník area. The storm is capable of producing brief heavy rain and occasional lightning as it tracks across the region. While not severe, conditions remain favorable for continued thunderstorm development along its path.

properties.text: A moderately strong thunderstorm with reflectivity around 55 dBZ is moving northeast across the Fours area. This intensity supports hail around 1–1.5 cm, mainly small but capable of briefly reaching the lower severe threshold if the core strengthens. Wind gusts may reach 60–75 km/h, especially on the forward‑flank side of the storm.

properties.text: A developing thunderstorm near Mělník with reflectivity around 50 dBZ is moving north‑northeast, producing bursts of heavy rain and occasional lightning. At this intensity, hail is generally 0.5–1 cm, with only a low chance of slightly larger stones if the core briefly strengthens. Wind gusts are likely in the 50–65 km/h range, strongest on the forward‑flank side of the storm.
